PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Maxwell Meyer Lantz, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner.

The court at the outset confirms with Mr. Lantz that he does not have any experience as an Administrator. Mr. Lantz offers oral testimony to confirm that he is aware of what his duties as Administrator will entail, especially regarding not making any distributions without prior court approval. The court also confirms that at this time, there is no plan to sell the real property.

The court then asks if there are any objections to this petition. No objections are made. Therefore, the Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed. The will dated Oct-31-2017 is admitted to probate. Charles Maxwell Meyer Lantz appointed Administrator with limited authority. No bond is required. Frank Mahoney, III is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Oct-25-2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-27-2021 Continued To Oct-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Sep-13-2021 Continued To Sep-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Maxwell Mever Lantz, via ZOOM, in propria-persona Petitioner.

The court begins by confirming that Mr. Lantz has seen the most recent Examiner's notes. The court then asks Mr. Lantz regarding the inconsistent dates in the will. Mr. Lantz reports that his parents had rushed to draft the will and signed it on OCT-30 but that his parents waited until OCT-31 to obtain witness signatures on the will. Mr. Meyer then reports that he has an inter-spousal property transfer document. The court asks Mr. Lantz to provide a copy of the inter-spousal property transfer document via email to the assigned Examiner, as soon as possible. The court advises Mr. Lantz that it is inclined to appoint him as Administrator with limited authority but would like to review the document regarding transfer, first.

The Petition for Probate of Will, etc., is continued to SEP-27-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Probate Examiner: Brandon Slykhous. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: F. Ruiz. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Aug-18-2021 Continued To Sep-13-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
